name: Shorty Rogers
description: American West coast jazz trumpeter, flugelhornist, and arranger (1924-1994)
abstract: Milton "Shorty" Rogers (born Milton Rajonsky; April 14, 1924 – November 7, 1994) was an American jazz musician, one of the principal creators of West Coast jazz. He played trumpet and flugelhorn and was in demand for his skills as an arranger.

works.t: Strike Up the Band: The Man I Love

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Wofford
about_quotes.evidence: And then shortly thereafter I worked with Shorty Rogers and his Giants which was a very well known band in those days.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Wofford interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: He was the child of Russian Jewish immigrants... One of his neighbors, whom he said he would hear him practice. He would play Sleepy Time Down South. That was a guy named Milton Rajonsky. Who is Milton Rajonsky? Shorty Rogers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: I had Shorty Rogers, Eddie Bert, Aaron Sax, Hank Kahout, Specs Powell, Clyde Lombardi. ... That was a wonderful little band, we had fun.

about_quotes.speaker: Akiyoshi Toshiko
about_quotes.evidence: he was one of the sweetest men that I probably have met... he came to visit me... we developed a lasting friendship
about_quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: Shorty and Marky Markowitz, me and Sonny was on the band at that time
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Poston
about_quotes.evidence: But Shorty Rogers, sweetest guy in the world, very easygoing... And over the years I would get to know many of those guys on a pretty close level. Because the type of events, the type of things that I was doing kind of required working closely with them, working closely with them on the music, thing
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Poston interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2024
